title: News Releases | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
body: News Releases Symptom-based approach treats opioid withdrawal in newborns with minimal drug exposure April 27, 2026 — NIH-funded clinical trial shows that the new approach could also decrease time in the hospital. Clinical trial finds no difference in fluid treatment options for pediatric sepsis April 24, 2026 — NIH-supported study is largest ever to compare fluid interventions to prevent major kidney damage in children treated for septic shock. NIH-funded AI model predicts cancer survival from single-cell tumor data April 21, 2026 — Study links specific cell populations to patient risk in melanoma and liver cancer. Dr. Jonathan M. Green selected as CEO of the NIH Clinical Center April 21, 2026 — The NIH Clinical Center is the largest research hospital in the world. NIH-funded breakthrough shrinks CRISPR for precision delivery in the body April 13, 2026 — Smaller gene-editing system could expand treatment options for cancer, ALS and other diseases. Low-cost care model reduces blood pressure in high-risk populations April 8, 2026 — NIH-supported clinical trial shows coordinated care strategy more effective than standard care NIH researchers discover pain-relieving drug with minimal addictive properties April 1, 2026 — Positive safety profile of novel drug compound is surprise for class of synthetic opioids shelved years ago. NIH awards top scientific teams for innovations linking nutrition and autoimmune disease March 30, 2026 — Competition sought bold ideas to better understand how dietary interventions could influence disease onset and symptom management. Dr. Elisabeth Armstrong named NIH Chief of Staff March 30, 2026 — She will provide strategic counsel to the NIH Director and other key leaders within NIH, in addition to managing process, operations, and information flows. Chronic inflammation leaves long-lasting impression on gut stem cells, increasing colorectal cancer risk March 25, 2026 — NIH-funded animal study finds heritable memories of damage persisted in cells months after inflammation ceased. For the Press Media Contacts NIH-Funded Research (EurekAlert.org) Pick-up Research Articles ( NIH Research Matters ) Pick-up Health Articles ( NIH News in Health ) More sources for NIH news and events Connect with Us Subscribe to news releases RSS Feed This page last reviewed on March 10, 2025
